body.u: An old man walked from his modern detached house, he didn’t bother to take his two year old family saloon, as the sun was brilliant on this certain August day. He was on his way to the corner shop, but as he stepped onto a zebra crossing a young learner motorcyclist of about 16 years old unwittingly knocked him over. The doctor said he was in a coma, but he was only dreaming of his life.
He’d passed all six of his exams and was soon settled into a nice college at ChatamChatham, where he me a nice girl from a neighbouring college.

At 18 he left college with an O.N.D. in building and soon got a job in an Estate Agency at Sheerness. He stayed at home for two years until he got a high position in the Surveying profession when he took out a morgagemortgage on a small, but modern semi-detached house.
He then went to live in this house with his girlfriend, future marriage partner.

With two wages coming in this bank balance rose dramatically and the morgagemortgage shrunk slowly but steadily. At 23 they married and in that same year he left the Estate agenceagency and joined an independent Chartered Surveyors firm which gave him an extra £30.00 a week extra. Soon they settled into a typical married life with a couple of kids, a small family car and a healthy bank balance. At 40 years old they moved to a detached house and brought a bigger car and then another. Soon the kids left home and at 53 he realised his life long ambition, that is, to own a boat and take people out fishing, for a price of course. In other words a charter boat. At 65 years old he died after a collision with a young motorcyclist. His wife said he always wanted to die suddenly without any pain or any prolonged suffering beforehand.
